compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:
ENDLESS Legend 2 Demo 
- Steam AppID of the game:
AppID: 3596660

Same issue reported to happen with actual game, AppID 3407390 here:
https://www.protondb.com/app/3407390

## System Information
- GPU: Intel Arc B580
- Video driver version: Mesa main
- Kernel version: 6.16.0+ (drm-tip)
- Proton version: Proton 9.0-4 and also happens with Proton experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Ground is not rendering correctly. This is likely because a tessellation shader fails to compile, more information and shader itself available in Mesa issue: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/issues/13694 .

## Reproduction

Launch game tutorial on Mesa release version. Debug version will hit asserts in spirv translation which indicates that either Proton has issue within spirv generation or game itself already has issue in the shader.
symptom_summary:
用户在运行《ENDLESS Legend 2 Demo》时遇到地面渲染不正确的问题，可能是由于曲面细分着色器无法编译所致。 

Tags: Rendering Issues, Shader Compilation, Visual Glitches

issue_title: Age of Empires IV: Anniversary Edition (1466860)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:Age of Empire IV
- Steam AppID of the game:1466860


## System Information
- GPU: Radeon R9 290/390
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 20.1.6
- Kernel Version: 5.13.0-27-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist] https://gist.github.com/jaafit/1ff149e605affbb3983a3255589a83c9
- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.

- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)--> [steam-1466860.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7938150/steam-1466860.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The app fails to launch as of today.  The game was working literally this morning.  After shutdown, moving the PC, and booting back up, it's not loading (it had been at least a week since reboot).  It gets as far as showing the Steam window "Preparing to launch Age of Empires IV" which goes away and then nothing.  

I tried reinstalling the game.  

warnings.log is interesting:
```
MapGen - Failed to load: attr->AttributeExists_PL(name) || attr->TableExists_PL(name)Failed loading an attribute data field for map generation. It is likely that your attribute data is out of sync with your local build or build from the build launcher.
[several lines like this]
....
(I) [19:38:01.772] [000000272]: Enumerating Graphics Adapters
(I) [19:38:01.786] [000000272]: D3DLOG Begin
(I) [19:38:01.786] [000000272]: D3DLOG End
(I) [19:38:01.786] [000000272]: Failed to create DXGI factory. HRESULT = 0x80004005 - Call failed.
Application crashed.
```
[end of file]

Another interesting change I noticed it that my window key is now working whereas this morning it was not.  Perhaps rebooting changed some configuration.
## Reproduction

Try to launch the app.
symptom_summary:
用户报告游戏《帝国时代 IV》无法启动，启动时显示“准备启动《帝国时代 IV》”的窗口后消失，随后没有任何反应。用户尝试重新安装游戏，但问题依旧存在，日志中显示与图形适配器相关的错误和应用崩溃信息。

**Tag Suggestions:**  
- Launch Failure  
- Application Crash  
- Missing Features

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: SAMURAI WARRIORS 5
- Steam AppID of the game: 1591530

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 470.86
- Kernel version: 5.13.0-25-generic
- Link to full system information report: [gist](https://gist.github.com/Meanmelter/113732769ae3c89c4eb51b34268bf320)
- Proton version: 6.3-8 && Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1591530.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8020815/steam-1591530.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game installs and launches with no issue. I can even load an initial menu! See screenshot below:
![Screenshot_20220207_233130](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17638040/152918862-41362dd3-447c-4be7-9edc-33f0eb9d3bf3.png)
After hitting any key, it goes to a black screen forever - I suspect it may be trying to play some kind of video.
I had installed Media Foundation workaround and while no video plays after that initial prompt, it seems I load into some kind of second menu, as my mouse clicks generate a sound effect sometimes yet the screen remains black.

## Reproduction
Install the game
Set Proton compatibility to 6.3-8 or Experimental
Launch the game
`<Workaround removed by moderator>`
Using a controller after the initial menu that loads seems to let you select SEVERAL options but none of them I can tell actually fix the black screen issue.
symptom_summary:
用户在启动《SAMURAI WARRIORS 5》后能够顺利进入初始菜单，但在按下任意键后屏幕变为黑色，且无法继续进行，尽管有时鼠标点击会产生声音效果。 

Tags: Black Screen, Menu Issues, Sound Effects

issue_title: 東方聖戦影 ～ Forlorn Souls of Wicked Past Demo (3138390)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: 東方聖戦影 ～ Forlorn Souls of Wicked Past Demo
- Steam AppID of the game: 3138390

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070
- Video driver version: nvidia 535.183.01
- Kernel version: Linux 6.8.0-48-generic x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): <https://gist.github.com/yoshiweegee/5f801b55450702417d15eca248d87bbc>
- Proton version: 7.0-6, 8.0-5, 9.0-3, "Experimental" (presumably [experimental-9.0 de3237c](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/commit/de3237cddf17ef20a8ad523794f46d1ec53258e2)), [experimental-9.0 de3237c](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/commit/de3237cddf17ef20a8ad523794f46d1ec53258e2) (explicitly built), [bleeding-edge 8f26364](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/commit/8f26364b0bee9448aba31ef3a0802403e5100c1b)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-3138390.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17510938/steam-3138390.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Shortly after the game window becomes visible and adjusts its dimensions, an error dialogue appears with a report. It sometimes appears behind the game window. Clicking OK allows the game to exit gracefully. The config program is unaffected.

On tested Proton versions from 9.0-3 to current bleeding-edge, the message is:
```
RenderShaderLibrary: Shader compile failed. [_HLSL_INTERNAL_RENDER2D]
        E_INVALIDARG
        E5020: The 'fx_2_0' target profile is only compatible with the 'fx' target type.
```
On tested earlier Proton versions, the message is:
```
RenderShaderLibrary: Shader compile failed. [_HLSL_INTERNAL_RENDER2D]
        E_FAIL
        <anonymous>:1:62: E5000: syntax error, unexpected ':', expecting ';' or ','
\
```
Both messages refer to the `_HLSL_INTERNAL_RENDER2D` shader defined within the game engine, ~~[Touhou Danmakufu ph3sx v1.33a-pre](https://github.com/Natashi/Touhou-Danmakufu-ph3sx-2/tree/v1.33a-pre)~~ [Touhou Danmakufu ph3sx zlabel v1.33a-pre (master)](https://github.com/nazjun/Touhou-Danmakufu-ph3sx-zlabel/tree/f96d73b0034445e7e32f676c8bed7243ac221871). The shader defined for v1.33a-pre can be found [here](https://github.com/nazjun/Touhou-Danmakufu-ph3sx-zlabel/blob/f96d73b0034445e7e32f676c8bed7243ac221871/source/GcLib/directx/HLSL.cpp#L10).

## Reproduction
1. Start the game (not the config program)
2. Wait for the window to become visible and adjust its dimensions
3. Observe the error dialogue. It may be hidden behind the game window.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
4. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
5. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
6. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
7. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Diagnostics` to
   the gist.
8. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
symptom_summary:
用户在启动游戏《東方聖戦影 ～ Forlorn Souls of Wicked Past Demo》后，游戏窗口显示后不久便出现错误对话框，提示着色器编译失败，导致游戏无法正常运行。点击确认后，游戏会优雅退出，但配置程序不受影响。

**Tag Suggestions:** Shader Compilation Error, Game Crash, Error Dialog

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Trinoline 
- Steam AppID of the game: 965010

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX Vega 56
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.3.7
- Kernel version: 5.16.14-200.fc35
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):
- Proton version: Proton Experimental & Stable

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)--> 


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Steam cloud doesn't work. Steam Cloud tries to sync the drive_c/users/steamuser/Documents/minori/TRINOLINE folder while the game uses drive_c/minori/TRINOLINE (Steam cloud works on Windows, the game isn't using the default path under Proton, a workaround is to symlink these two folder)

## Reproduction
Launch the game, start playing then save.
